finding: tiers.json says team tier is 'Lightweight + Claude Code hooks, startup script, and claudeignore'; tiers.md, README.md and team-tier.md say team tier is 'Lightweight + GitHub Issues backend + parallel worktrees with gate-enforced merge'.
detail: install.py --tier team projects only Claude Code integration files; no GitHub Issues code or worktree machinery is added.
recommendation: Either extend the team tier projections (Issues adapter + worktree plumbing) or rewrite README/tiers.md/team-tier.md to describe team tier as 'Claude Code integration + opt-in github-issues configuration'.

finding: ADR-022 names hedl.toml as where state_backend lives; code, tests, installer and docs all use .work/context.json.
detail: ADR-022 puts the same field in a different file from every implementation artefact.
recommendation: Amend ADR-022 to match the shipped implementation (.work/context.json) or migrate the implementation; pick one and update the other.

finding: tiers.md says lightweight ships 'all 15 command behaviors'; commands.md contains 18 distinct behavior sections.
evidence: skill/hedl/references/tiers.md:78; skill/hedl/references/commands.md ## headings (excluding the new-decision ADR-template sub-headings): 18
recommendation: Update tiers.md or generate the count programmatically from commands.md.

finding: review-dispatcher.md lists determinism-auditor under 'reference library agents' (to be instantiated from review-library.md), but determinism-auditor is a named file in skill/hedl/agents/ and has no prompt in review-library.md.
evidence: skill/hedl/agents/review-dispatcher.md:36-37,60; skill/hedl/agents/determinism-auditor.md; docs/agents.md:42-52; review-library.md has no '## determinism-auditor' section
detail: A dispatcher faithfully following the table would fail to load a prompt that does not exist in the library.
recommendation: Move determinism-auditor into the 'core agents (named files)' table in review-dispatcher.md.

finding: ADR-011 requires README to open with 'Don't use Hedl if...' and that docs/alternatives.md exist; the README has no such section and docs/alternatives.md does not exist.
detail: ADR-011 is Accepted (Phase 0). ADR-012 also depends on docs/alternatives.md.
recommendation: Either implement the disqualifier section and create docs/alternatives.md, or move ADR-011/012 to 'Accepted (deferred)' with rationale.

finding: standards.md defines 'workstream' as one of four fixed categories (WS-PLAN/WS-REQ/WS-TECH/WS-ARCH); ADR-019 defines 'workstream' as a recursive unit of tracked work that subsumes phase and work-item.
detail: The same noun denotes two incompatible things in shipped files; iterate.md branches on item.workstream as a category.
recommendation: Resolve via the ADR-019 terminology audit before either definition is taken as authoritative (rename the categories or fold them into the recursive model).

finding: standards.md says a CI workflow named 'PR Template Check' enforces the PR template; no such workflow exists. The check runs inside am_i_done.py.
evidence: skill/hedl/references/standards.md:74-75; .github/workflows/ contains only am-i-done.yml and codeql.yml; .github/scripts/am_i_done.py:857 invokes check_pr_template.py
recommendation: Reword standards.md to attribute the check to the am-i-done workflow / am_i_done gate sub-check.

finding: CLAUDE.md states 'Phase: 0 — Setup' but .work/phases/phase-0.json is byte-identical to the EDIT-ME template ('Project Phase 0', placeholder goal/started/DoD).
recommendation: Author a real Phase 0 in phase-0.json or correct CLAUDE.md to say Phase 0 has not been authored.

finding: CLAUDE.md Principle 4 ('One work item at a time') and ADR-018 ('clarified as one per operator') disagree; CLAUDE.md was not updated.
recommendation: Update CLAUDE.md Principle 4 or drop the CLAUDE.md edit claim from ADR-018. (Already tracked as WORK-0009.)
